markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define the Handshake job that detects contradictions, stale facts, granularity mismatches, and temporal conflicts. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: ConflictDetectionAgentJob leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ConflictDetectionAgentJob is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 91c204e6; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-122 requires a Handshake-native ConflictDetectionAgentJob backed by PostgreSQL/EventLedger that detects claim conflicts and records them as durable authority with receipts. The commit (91c204e6) delivers a deterministic symbolic conflict-detection job (SQL self-join candidate search -> committed knowledge_claim_conflicts -> typed detection-job record), real migration 0242 with FK to kernel_event_ledger and CHECK constraints, and integration tests against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (port 5544) with no SQLite/mock fallback. Code implements the MT and aligns with current Master Spec v02.194 section 2.3.13.11.
findings: src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_memory/conflict.rs:run_symbolic_conflict_detection - real orchestration: runs deterministic candidate search, records committed knowledge_claim_conflict per pair (moving both backing claims to `conflicted` via the 0137 substrate), files a typed detection-job record; idempotent via claims_already_conflicting() guard. No placeholders/escapes.

src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge_memory.rs:67 find_fact_conflict_candidates - real SQL self-join (same subject_entity_id+predicate_key, object IS DISTINCT FROM) ordered a.fact_id<b.fact_id, LIMIT-bounded; deterministic and replayable.

migrations/0242_knowledge_memory_conflict_jobs.sql - real DDL: detection-jobs, findings, resolution-jobs tables with FK to kernel_event_ledger(event_id), CHECK on outcome/claim-shape, knowledge_schema_registry rows marked authority_class='authority'; down migration present (0242...down.sql).

tests/knowledge_memory_conflict_tests.rs - 3 #[tokio::test] cases exercise real PG: detection_job_records_conflict_and_is_idempotent asserts both claims -> KnowledgeClaimState::Conflicted and re-run records 0 new conflicts; resolution test appends a real kernel_event_ledger receipt and asserts two negative fail-closed paths (StorageError::Validation).

tests/knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs + knowledge_pg_support.rs - proof path is Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres::ensure_running, default port 5544) or operator POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL; explicitly NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (tests SKIP loudly when binaries absent).

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2704 mandates KnowledgeClaim lifecycle incl `conflicted` + conflict refs + resolution receipts and PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority (2711/2713) - implementation matches exactly.
Self-cert: MT-122 lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=null, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION - not self-certified COMPLETED (sub-rule 3 satisfied).

Honest scoping aside (not a fail): only the `symbolic` detection_kind has a runtime search path; temporal/alias/stale_source/granularity/semantic exist as enum/CHECK values reserved for future extension. The contract explicitly notes embedding/semantic is a future runtime concern; no live code path returns a placeholder for the unimplemented kinds, so this is honest deferral, not a deferred-live escape.
Paperwork aside: packet spec_anchors cite v02.192 and packet still lists status PENDING acceptance rows - expected during governance wind-down, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
